baystreetcrypto.com · A field guide

Crypto for Canadians, by Canadians

Tax guides, exchange reviews, ETF analysis, and regulatory updates tailored specifically for the Canadian cryptocurrency market.

Edition 2026 Last revised July 2026

Contents

01

Introduction

Canada has one of the most developed cryptocurrency markets in the world — home to the first Bitcoin ETF, a clear (if complex) tax framework, and abundant cheap energy for mining. But navigating the Canadian crypto landscape requires Canada-specific knowledge. CRA rules aren't the same as IRS rules. Our exchanges aren't the same as American ones. This site covers everything Canadian crypto investors need to know.

02

Canadian crypto topics

Canadian Crypto Tax Guide

Tax

The CRA treats cryptocurrency as a commodity, making every disposal a taxable event. Our guide covers capital gains vs. business income classification, cost basis tracking, TFSA and RRSP implications, and common filing mistakes Canadians make.

    Capital gains vs. business income rules,ACB (Adjusted Cost Base) calculation,TFSA and RRSP crypto ETF strategies,CRA reporting requirements for crypto,Common audit triggers to avoid
Read tax guide →

Best Canadian Exchanges

Exchanges

Not all crypto exchanges are legal in Canada. We review CSA-registered platforms — comparing fees, available coins, CAD funding options, security practices, and customer support for Canadian users.

    CSA registration status verified,CAD deposit and withdrawal options,Trading fee comparisons,Available cryptocurrencies,Security and insurance coverage
Compare exchanges →

Crypto ETFs on TSX

Investing

Canada was the first country to approve spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs. We analyze the available TSX-listed crypto ETFs — management fees (MERs), holdings, tax treatment, and whether they belong in your TFSA, RRSP, or taxable account.

    All TSX-listed crypto ETF profiles,MER comparison and fee analysis,TFSA vs. RRSP tax implications,Spot vs. futures-based ETF differences,Performance tracking and benchmarking
Explore ETFs →

Mining in Canada

Mining

Canada's abundant hydroelectric power and cold climate make it ideal for cryptocurrency mining. We cover the economics of mining operations, provincial energy rates, regulatory requirements, and environmental considerations.

    Provincial energy rate comparisons,Mining profitability calculators,Regulatory requirements by province,Environmental and sustainability factors,Home mining vs. hosted mining options
Learn about mining →

Canadian Regulations

Regulation

Canada's crypto regulatory framework involves federal and provincial bodies — CSA, FINTRAC, OSFI, and more. We break down what's regulated, what's coming, and how Canadian rules compare to other jurisdictions.

    CSA registration requirements,FINTRAC money services business rules,Provincial securities commission roles,Upcoming regulatory proposals,Canada vs. US regulatory comparison
Understand regulations →

Quebec Hydro Mining

Mining

Quebec's Hydro-Quebec offers some of the cheapest electricity in North America, making it a global hotspot for Bitcoin mining. We cover current rates, Hydro-Quebec's policies on crypto miners, and how to set up operations in the province.

    Hydro-Quebec rate structures for miners,Application process and requirements,Cold climate cooling advantages,Environmental impact and green mining,Major mining operations in Quebec
Explore Quebec mining →
03

Getting started with crypto in Canada

Choose a registered Canadian exchange

Canadian Securities Administrators (CSA) require crypto trading platforms to register. Using a registered exchange ensures your platform meets Canadian regulatory standards and your assets have some baseline protections.

Understand your CRA obligations

The CRA treats crypto as a commodity. Every trade, swap, and spend is a taxable event. Keep meticulous records from day one — retroactively tracking cost basis across multiple exchanges and wallets is a nightmare.

Consider crypto ETFs for simplicity

Canada pioneered spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, available on the TSX. If you want crypto exposure without managing wallets and keys, ETFs in your TFSA or RRSP can be a tax-efficient option.

Explore mining opportunities

Canada's cheap hydroelectric power — especially in Quebec, Manitoba, and British Columbia — makes it one of the best countries in the world for Bitcoin mining. Understand the regulatory and energy landscape before investing in hardware.

04

Canadian crypto resources

CRA Cryptocurrency Guide

The official Canada Revenue Agency guidance on how cryptocurrency is taxed, including capital gains, business income, and mining income. Read CRA guide

CSA Registered Platforms

The Canadian Securities Administrators maintain a list of registered and pre-registered crypto trading platforms operating legally in Canada. View registered platforms

TMX Group (TSX)

The Toronto Stock Exchange lists multiple crypto ETFs. Research available products directly on the TMX website. Visit TMX

05

Questions, answered

Bay Street Crypto

BayStreetCrypto.com provides Canadian cryptocurrency market analysis and educational content. This is not financial or tax advice. Consult a qualified Canadian tax professional for your specific situation. We may earn commissions through affiliate links.
